description:
You are a platform engineer acting as the on-call Kubernetes operator.  
    A staging area holding the latest cluster manifests has already been mounted for you at  
    
        /opt/remote_manifests  
    
    It currently contains exactly three files:  
    
        deployment.yaml  
        service.yaml  
        configmap.yaml  
    
    Your goal is to synchronise these manifests into your personal workspace under  
    
        /home/user/work/manifests  
    
    and then record a short report about what was transferred.

    Required end-state of the system
    --------------------------------
    1. The directory  /home/user/work/manifests  must exist.  
       • It must contain **only** the three files listed above.  
       • Each file’s byte-for-byte contents must be identical to the counterpart that remains in /opt/remote_manifests.  

    2. A plain-text file called  /home/user/sync_report.log  must be created.  
       • It must contain one line per manifest file, in **alphabetical order**, using the following exact format (no extra spaces or blank lines):  
       
           <filename>: <md5sum-of-local-copy>  

         For example, the first line in the finished log **must** literally start with  
         
             configmap.yaml:  

         followed by a single space and the 32-character hexadecimal MD5 digest of /home/user/work/manifests/configmap.yaml, then a newline.  
       • Only the three manifest entries must appear, and nothing else.  

    3. File ownership and permissions do not need to be changed; default user-level permissions are sufficient.  

    Constraints & hints (not commands)
    ----------------------------------
    • Use rsync in archive/verbose mode so that timestamps and permissions are preserved.  
    • Calculate each file’s MD5 sum **after** the synchronisation is complete so the checksum reflects the local copy.  
    • Remember to create the workspace directory before running rsync, or use rsync’s “create-dirs” capability.  
    • The MD5 utility available in all common Linux images is `md5sum`.  
    • No network access or elevated (root) privileges are required.

    What the automated grader will check
    ------------------------------------
    1. Existence of /home/user/work/manifests and that it contains exactly three files with the correct names.  
    2. A byte-level comparison between each file in /home/user/work/manifests and its peer in /opt/remote_manifests.  
    3. Presence and exact content of /home/user/sync_report.log, verifying:  
       • Alphabetical ordering:  configmap.yaml, deployment.yaml, service.yaml  
       • Proper “filename: md5hash” format (single space after the colon, 32 lowercase hexadecimal characters).  
       • The MD5 hashes recorded match the ones calculated by the grader from the synced files.  

    Reminder: do **not** delete or modify anything inside /opt/remote_manifests; copy/synchronise only.
truth:
Initial file hierarchy that exists **before** the task starts
    -------------------------------------------------------------
    /opt/remote_manifests/
    ├── configmap.yaml
    ├── deployment.yaml
    └── service.yaml

    Full, exact textual contents (each file terminates with a single trailing newline):

    1. /opt/remote_manifests/configmap.yaml
       --------------------------------------------------
       apiVersion: v1
       kind: ConfigMap
       metadata:
         name: app-config
       data:
         LOG_LEVEL: "info"

    2. /opt/remote_manifests/deployment.yaml
       --------------------------------------------------
       apiVersion: apps/v1
       kind: Deployment
       metadata:
         name: web
       spec:
         replicas: 3
         selector:
           matchLabels:
             app: web
         template:
           metadata:
             labels:
               app: web
           spec:
             containers:
             - name: nginx
               image: nginx:1.25
               ports:
               - containerPort: 80

    3. /opt/remote_manifests/service.yaml
       --------------------------------------------------
       apiVersion: v1
       kind: Service
       metadata:
         name: web
       spec:
         type: ClusterIP
         selector:
           app: web
         ports:
         - port: 80
           targetPort: 80

    The test harness computes the reference MD5 checksums for the above three files at runtime and stores them in variables
      REF_MD5_CONFIGMAP, REF_MD5_DEPLOYMENT, REF_MD5_SERVICE
    for comparison against the entries in /home/user/sync_report.log.

    Verification logic (performed after agent finishes)
    ----------------------------------------------------
    1. Assert directory /home/user/work/manifests exists.
    2. Assert it contains exactly three files: configmap.yaml, deployment.yaml, service.yaml.
    3. For each of those files:
         a. Compute md5sum of local copy.
         b. Compute md5sum of corresponding source file in /opt/remote_manifests.
         c. Both must be equal.
    4. Read /home/user/sync_report.log and ensure it consists of **exactly** three lines:
         Line 1  ==  "configmap.yaml: ${REF_MD5_CONFIGMAP}\n"
         Line 2  ==  "deployment.yaml: ${REF_MD5_DEPLOYMENT}\n"
         Line 3  ==  "service.yaml: ${REF_MD5_SERVICE}\n"
       (Where `${REF_MD5_*}` are the runtime-computed 32-char hashes, and “\n” denotes a single newline.)
